title: Anisomeris oligantha
intro: Anisomeris oligantha, commonly known as Mexican tea or Yerba de la Santa Cruz, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and numerous medicinal properties. The health benefits of Anisomeris oligantha include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Anisomeris oligantha is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to reap its therapeutic benefits and promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Anisomeris oligantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Capparaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is further classified as a member of the phylum Magnoliophyta. Within the family Capparaceae, Anisomeris oligantha is part of the subfamily Capparoideae. Anisomeris oligantha is a small shrub native to Mexico. This plant is also known as the Mexican caper or desert caper.
